input.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Docker Stats Demonstration
# This script shows various ways to use docker stats command

echo "=========================================="
echo "Docker Stats - Container Resource Usage"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

echo "1. BASIC DOCKER STATS COMMAND"
echo "   Command: docker stats"
echo "   Description: Display resource usage for all running containers in real-time"
echo "   Output includes:"
echo "     - CONTAINER ID: Container identifier"
echo "     - NAME: Container name"
echo "     - CPU %: CPU usage percentage"
echo "     - MEM USAGE / LIMIT: Memory usage vs available"
echo "     - MEM %: Memory usage percentage"
echo "     - NET I/O: Network input/output"
echo "     - BLOCK I/O: Disk input/output"
echo ""

echo "2. VIEW STATS FOR SPECIFIC CONTAINER"
echo "   Command: docker stats container_name"
echo "   Description: Show stats for only one specific container"
echo ""

echo "3. DISPLAY STATS WITHOUT STREAMING (SNAPSHOT)"
echo "   Command: docker stats --no-stream"
echo "   Description: Show current stats once and exit (non-interactive)"
echo ""

echo "4. VIEW STATS FOR MULTIPLE CONTAINERS"
echo "   Command: docker stats container1 container2 container3"
echo "   Description: Show stats for specific containers only"
echo ""

echo "5. DISABLE STREAMING WITH DETAILED OUTPUT"
echo "   Command: docker stats --no-stream --format \"table {{.Container}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\""
echo "   Description: Custom formatting of the output"
echo ""

echo "6. COMMON FORMAT OPTIONS"
echo "   - {{.Container}}: Container name/ID"
echo "   - {{.CPUPerc}}: CPU percentage"
echo "   - {{.MemUsage}}: Memory usage"
echo "   - {{.MemPerc}}: Memory percentage"
echo "   - {{.NetInput}}: Network input"
echo "   - {{.NetOutput}}: Network output"
echo "   - {{.BlockInput}}: Disk input"
echo "   - {{.BlockOutput}}: Disk output"
echo "   - {{.PIDs}}: Number of processes"
echo ""

echo "7. EXAMPLE CUSTOM FORMAT"
echo "   docker stats --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}'"
echo "   This displays: Container Names | CPU % | Memory Usage"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "DOCKER STATS OPTIONS"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
echo "  -a, --all              Show all containers (default shows only running)"
echo "  --no-stream            Disable streaming stats and only pull the first result"
echo "  --format string        Pretty-print containers stats to a Go template"
echo "  --no-trunc             Don't truncate output"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE USE CASES"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

echo "USE CASE 1: Monitor CPU-intensive containers"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream | sort -k 3 -rn | head"
echo ""

echo "USE CASE 2: Export stats to CSV"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream --format='{{.Container}},{{.CPUPerc}},{{.MemUsage}}' > stats.csv"
echo ""

echo "USE CASE 3: Monitor memory usage and sort by memory"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream | sort -k 5 -rn | head"
echo ""

echo "USE CASE 4: Real-time monitoring with custom format"
echo "  docker stats --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}'"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "INTERPRETING THE OUTPUT"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
echo "CPU %: Percentage of host CPU being used by the container"
echo "MEM USAGE: Current memory usage of the container"
echo "MEM LIMIT: Maximum memory the container can use"
echo "MEM %: Percentage of allocated memory being used"
echo "NET I/O: Bytes sent/received on network interfaces"
echo "BLOCK I/O: Bytes read/written to block devices (disk)"
echo "PIDs: Number of processes running in the container"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "TIPS & TRICKS"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
echo "• Use Ctrl+C to stop the streaming output"
echo "• Combine with watch command: watch 'docker stats --no-stream'"
echo "• Use grep to filter specific containers: docker stats | grep 'web-server'"
echo "• Export metrics for monitoring tools: docker stats --no-stream > metrics.txt"
echo "• Monitor trends with: docker stats --format json (useful for parsing)"
echo ""

#!/bin/bash

# Practical Docker Stats Examples
# These commands can be run in a Docker environment

echo "=========================================="
echo "PRACTICAL DOCKER STATS EXAMPLES"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""

echo "EXAMPLE 1: Get a single snapshot of all running containers"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output Format:"
echo "CONTAINER ID   NAME           CPU %     MEM USAGE / LIMIT     MEM %     NET I/O           BLOCK I/O      PIDs"
echo "abc123def456   webserver      0.05%     256MiB / 1GiB        25.60%    1.23kB / 456B     0B / 0B        15"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 2: Monitor specific container continuously"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats webserver"
echo ""
echo "This will update every second and show real-time metrics"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 3: Get stats for multiple specific containers"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats webserver database cache"
echo ""
echo "Shows stats for only these three containers"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 4: Custom format - Container name and CPU usage"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}'"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output:"
echo "NAMES                CPU %"
echo "webserver            0.05%"
echo "database             0.12%"
echo "cache                0.02%"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 5: Custom format - Comprehensive overview"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\t{{.PIDs}}'"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output:"
echo "NAMES        CPU %    MEM %     MEM USAGE       PIDs"
echo "webserver    0.05%    25.60%    256MiB / 1GiB   15"
echo "database     0.12%    45.30%    453MiB / 1GiB   23"
echo "cache        0.02%    10.20%    102MiB / 1GiB   8"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 6: Show all containers (including stopped)"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --all --no-stream"
echo ""
echo "This includes stopped containers with 0 resource usage"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 7: Find containers using most CPU (one-time)"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream | sort -k3 -rn | head -5"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output: Top 5 CPU consumers"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 8: Find containers using most memory (one-time)"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream | sort -k5 -rn | head -5"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output: Top 5 memory consumers"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 9: Export stats to a file"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream > container_stats.txt"
echo ""
echo "Saves current snapshot to a file for later analysis"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 10: Monitor with continuous refresh (like top)"
echo "Command:"
echo "  watch -n 2 'docker stats --no-stream'"
echo ""
echo "Updates every 2 seconds, use 'q' to quit"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 11: JSON output for programmatic parsing"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream --format json | jq '.'"
echo ""
echo "Output is JSON format, easy to parse with jq or other tools"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 12: Network I/O statistics"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.NetInput}}\t{{.NetOutput}}'"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output:"
echo "NAMES        NET INPUT    NET OUTPUT"
echo "webserver    1.23MB       456KB"
echo "database     234MB        789KB"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "EXAMPLE 13: Disk I/O statistics"
echo "Command:"
echo "  docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.BlockInput}}\t{{.BlockOutput}}'"
echo ""
echo "Expected Output:"
echo "NAMES        BLOCK INPUT   BLOCK OUTPUT"
echo "webserver    0B            0B"
echo "database     1.2GB         567MB"
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "SHELL SCRIPT: Monitor and alert on high CPU"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
cat << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# This script monitors containers and alerts if CPU exceeds threshold

THRESHOLD=80
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}' | tail -n +2 | while read line; do
    container=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}')
    cpu=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/%//')
    
    if (( $(echo "$cpu > $THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
        echo "ALERT: Container $container is using ${cpu}% CPU!"
    fi
done
EOF
echo ""

echo "=========================================="
echo "SHELL SCRIPT: Log stats periodically"
echo "=========================================="
echo ""
cat << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Log container stats every 5 minutes

LOG_FILE="/var/log/docker_stats.log"

while true; do
    {
        echo "=== $(date) ==="
        docker stats --no-stream
        echo ""
    } >> "$LOG_FILE"
    sleep 300  # Wait 5 minutes
done
EOF
echo ""

input.content:
# Docker Stats - Complete Reference Guide

## Overview
`docker stats` displays a live stream of container(s) resource usage statistics. It shows real-time information about CPU, memory, network I/O, and block device I/O for running containers.

## Basic Syntax
```bash
docker stats [OPTIONS] [CONTAINER...]
```

## Core Options

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--all, -a` | Show all containers (default shows only running) |
| `--no-stream` | Disable streaming stats (show snapshot and exit) |
| `--format string` | Pretty-print containers using a Go template |
| `--no-trunc` | Don't truncate output |

## Output Fields Explained

### CONTAINER ID / NAME
- The unique identifier or name of the container

### CPU %
- **Percentage of host CPU** being used by the container
- Based on the number of CPUs on the host machine
- Example: On a 4-core machine, max is ~400%

### MEM USAGE / LIMIT
- **Current memory usage** vs **memory limit** of the container
- Format: `256MiB / 1GiB`
- Set with `docker run -m` or `--memory` flag

### MEM %
- **Percentage of allocated memory** being used
- Calculated as: (MEM USAGE / LIMIT) × 100

### NET I/O
- **Network input / output bytes** since container start
- Format: `1.23kB / 456B` (sent / received)
- Resets when container restarts

### BLOCK I/O
- **Block device I/O bytes** (disk read/write) since container start
- Format: `0B / 0B` (read / write)
- Used for storage operations

### PIDs
- **Number of processes** running inside the container
- Useful for identifying process leaks

---

## Format Templates

### Available Template Variables
```
{{.Container}}     - Container ID (short)
{{.ContainerFull}} - Container ID (full)
{{.Names}}         - Container name
{{.CPUPerc}}       - CPU percentage
{{.MemUsage}}      - Memory usage
{{.MemLimit}}      - Memory limit
{{.MemPerc}}       - Memory percentage
{{.NetInput}}      - Network input
{{.NetOutput}}     - Network output
{{.BlockInput}}    - Block input (disk read)
{{.BlockOutput}}   - Block output (disk write)
{{.PIDs}}          - Number of processes
```

### Example Format Strings

**Compact view - Name and CPU:**
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}'
```

**Detailed view - All metrics:**
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}\t{{.PIDs}}'
```

**CSV format:**
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format '{{.Names}},{{.CPUPerc}},{{.MemUsage}},{{.NetInput}},{{.NetOutput}}'
```

**Custom separator (colon):**
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format '{{.Names}}:{{.CPUPerc}}:{{.MemUsage}}'
```

---

## Common Use Cases

### 1. Get a Single Snapshot (Non-Interactive)
```bash
docker stats --no-stream
```
Shows current stats once and returns to prompt.

### 2. Monitor One Container Continuously
```bash
docker stats webserver
```
Updates every second until Ctrl+C.

### 3. Monitor Multiple Specific Containers
```bash
docker stats web1 web2 database
```

### 4. Show All Containers Including Stopped
```bash
docker stats --all --no-stream
```

### 5. Find Top CPU Consumers
```bash
docker stats --no-stream | sort -k3 -rn | head -5
```

### 6. Find Top Memory Consumers
```bash
docker stats --no-stream | sort -k5 -rn | head -5
```

### 7. Export Stats to File
```bash
docker stats --no-stream > container_stats.txt
```

### 8. Continuous Monitoring with Refresh (like `top`)
```bash
watch -n 2 'docker stats --no-stream'
```
Updates every 2 seconds.

### 9. Get Stats in JSON Format
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format json
```

### 10. Parse with JSON Query Tool
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format json | jq '.[] | {name: .Names, cpu: .CPUPerc, memory: .MemUsage}'
```

---

## Advanced Examples

### Memory Usage Monitoring with Alerts
```bash
#!/bin/bash
THRESHOLD=80

docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.MemPerc}}' | tail -n +2 | while read line; do
    container=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}')
    mem=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/%//')
    
    if (( $(echo "$mem > $THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
        echo "ALERT: $container memory at ${mem}%"
        # Send notification, restart container, etc.
    fi
done
```

### Periodic Logging Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG_FILE="/var/log/docker_stats.log"

while true; do
    {
        echo "=== $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') ==="
        docker stats --no-stream
        echo ""
    } >> "$LOG_FILE"
    sleep 300  # Log every 5 minutes
done
```

### Monitor Network Activity
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.NetInput}}\t{{.NetOutput}}'
```

### Monitor Disk I/O
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.BlockInput}}\t{{.BlockOutput}}'
```

### Check Process Count Leaks
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.PIDs}}' | sort -k2 -rn
```

---

## Performance Considerations

### Impact on System
- **Low overhead**: Minimal CPU impact
- **Real-time data**: Updates every ~100ms per container
- **No persistent storage**: Data is not persisted (use monitoring tools for that)

### Tips for Large Deployments
- Use `--no-stream` for batch operations
- Filter specific containers instead of monitoring all
- Redirect output to files for log retention
- Consider using monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana) for historical data

---

## Interpreting High Values

### High CPU %
- Container is CPU-bound
- Check application logs for infinite loops or stuck processes
- Consider increasing CPU quota or optimizing the application

### High Memory %
- Container is memory-constrained
- May have memory leaks
- Consider increasing memory limit or optimizing code
- Check for unbounded caches or data structures

### High Network I/O
- Container is network-intensive
- Check for data exfiltration or excessive API calls
- Monitor bandwidth usage on the host

### High Block I/O
- Container is doing heavy disk operations
- Check for logging, database operations, or file processing
- Monitor disk I/O on the host

### High PIDs
- Many processes running
- Could indicate process leaks if steadily increasing
- Check for fork bombs or zombie processes

---

## Troubleshooting

### Permission Denied
```bash
# Solution 1: Use sudo
sudo docker stats

# Solution 2: Add user to docker group
usermod -aG docker $USER
```

### No Output (No Running Containers)
```bash
# List all containers
docker ps -a

# Start a container
docker run -d nginx

# Then run stats
docker stats --no-stream
```

### Stats Not Updating
- Check if container is actually running: `docker ps`
- Verify container is using resources (CPU, memory)
- Try with `--no-stream` flag

### Memory Shows 0 %
- Container doesn't have memory limit set
- Set with: `docker run -m 512m ...`
- Shows `--` in output when no limit is set

---

## Integration with Other Tools

### Combine with grep
```bash
docker stats --no-stream | grep webserver
```

### Combine with awk
```bash
docker stats --no-stream | awk 'NR>1 {print $2, $4}'
```

### Pipe to grep and sort
```bash
docker stats --no-stream | grep web | sort -k5 -rn
```

### Use with xargs to stop high-CPU containers
```bash
docker stats --no-stream | sort -k3 -rn | head -2 | awk 'NR>1 {print $2}' | xargs -I {} docker stop {}
```

---

## Comparison with Other Tools

| Tool | Purpose | Advantage |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `docker stats` | Quick resource check | Built-in, no dependencies |
| `docker top` | Show running processes | See individual processes |
| `docker inspect` | Container configuration | Detailed config info |
| `Prometheus` | Long-term monitoring | Historical data, alerts |
| `cAdvisor` | Container metrics | Advanced analysis |
| `Grafana` | Visualization | Beautiful dashboards |

---

## Best Practices

1. **Monitor regularly**: Use cron jobs or background scripts
2. **Set memory limits**: Use `--memory` when running containers
3. **Log metrics**: Save output to files for historical analysis
4. **Use alerts**: Script thresholds for CPU/memory usage
5. **Monitor trends**: Not just snapshots, but changes over time
6. **Test under load**: Know your containers' normal resource usage
7. **Keep logs**: Rotate stats logs like you do with application logs

---

## Command Quick Reference

```bash
# Basic usage
docker stats                              # Stream all containers
docker stats container_name              # Stream one container
docker stats --no-stream                 # Get snapshot and exit

# Show all containers
docker stats --all --no-stream            # Include stopped containers

# Custom formatting
docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}'

# Find resource hogs
docker stats --no-stream | sort -k3 -rn | head -5   # Top 5 CPU users
docker stats --no-stream | sort -k5 -rn | head -5   # Top 5 Memory users

# Export and analyze
docker stats --no-stream > stats.txt     # Save to file
docker stats --no-stream --format json | jq '.'  # JSON output

# Monitor with refresh
watch -n 2 'docker stats --no-stream'    # Refresh every 2 seconds
```

---

## Related Commands

- `docker top CONTAINER` - Show running processes in a container
- `docker inspect CONTAINER` - Display container configuration
- `docker ps` - List containers
- `docker logs CONTAINER` - View container logs
- `docker exec CONTAINER` - Run commands in a container
